title: Ohio City Apartments for Rent
metaTitle: Ohio City Apartments for Rent in Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Ohio City apartments for rent near the West Side Market and RTA Red Line. See rent ranges, transit, and voucher-friendly options. Book a free showing today.
answerBox: Ohio City is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood in ZIP 44113, home to the West Side Market and served by RTA's Red Line at the Ohio City station. Apartment rents here generally track at or above the citywide median of $1,250/month (Zumper, July 2026), and we work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around the neighborhood.

sections.h2: Renting an apartment in Ohio City
body: Ohio City sits on Cleveland's near-west side, in **ZIP code 44113**, directly across the Cuyahoga River from downtown. It's the location of the historic **West Side Market**, one of Cleveland's oldest continuously operating public markets, and it's served directly by GCRTA's **Red Line at the Ohio City station**. Housing stock here mixes late-1800s Victorian-era houses and rowhouses, some converted into multi-unit apartments, alongside newer infill apartment buildings near the market district.

We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City as part of our broader Cleveland selection — [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see what's currently available, or [tell our leasing team](/p/book-showing) what size and budget you need.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent an apartment in Ohio City?
body: Ohio City's proximity to downtown and the West Side Market district tends to put its apartment pricing at or above Cleveland's citywide averages. Per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, the citywide median rent runs from $1,060 for a studio to $1,350 for a three-bedroom, while RentCafe (Yardi)'s July 2, 2026 report — which tracks larger, professionally managed buildings — shows meaningfully higher averages at every size. Near-west side neighborhoods like Ohio City, with a mix of historic conversions and newer construction, typically land within or above these citywide ranges depending on the specific building.

Ohio City is one of the few Cleveland neighborhoods with its own rapid-transit stop: the **Ohio City station on RTA's Red Line**, which runs to downtown Tower City in one direction and to Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the city's east side (including University Circle and Windermere) in the other. West 25th Street is the neighborhood's main commercial corridor, running past the West Side Market, Market Square Park, and the Hingetown area, with I-90 access nearby.

items: **ZIP code:** 44113, near-west side, across the river from downtown
**Transit:** RTA Red Line, Ohio City station; I-90 access nearby
**Landmarks:** West Side Market, Market Square Park, W. 25th Street corridor
**Housing stock:** Late-1800s houses and rowhouses, many converted to apartments, plus newer infill construction

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers and Ohio City apartments
body: Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) are administered countywide by the **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)** through a year-round preliminary application and mini-lottery selection process, regardless of which Cleveland neighborhood a household chooses. As with the rest of the City of Cleveland, Ohio City has no local ordinance requiring landlords to accept vouchers — Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, and Cleveland has not enacted its own — so acceptance depends on the individual landlord. Every rental home we work with in and around Ohio City accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready; see our [full Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the CMHA process works.

sections.h2: Finding an apartment in Ohio City
body: Start by [booking a free showing](/p/book-showing) so our leasing team can walk you through what's currently available in and around Ohio City. If you'd like to compare Ohio City to Cleveland's nearby near-west side neighborhood, see our [Tremont rental guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-tremont-cleveland/), or browse [apartments across Cleveland by area and budget](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/). When you've found a fit, [apply online](/apply) to move forward.

As with any Cleveland-area rental, our application process reviews income relative to rent, rental history, and any voucher or co-signer support you can bring — the same consistent process applies whether you're touring a unit in Ohio City or elsewhere in our available homes. Bringing pay stubs, landlord references, or voucher paperwork to your showing can help speed up a decision once you've picked a home.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with apartments in Ohio City?
faq.a: Yes. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City as part of our broader Cleveland-area selection. Book a free showing to see current availability, since specific units change regularly.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent in Ohio City?
faq.a: Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts median rents from $1,060 (studio) to $1,350 (3-bedroom), while RentCafe's same-period average for professionally managed buildings runs higher at every size. Ohio City, close to downtown and the West Side Market, typically prices within or above these citywide ranges.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Ohio City in?
faq.a: Ohio City is in ZIP code 44113, on Cleveland's near-west side, directly across the Cuyahoga River from downtown.

Ohio City has its own RTA Red Line station, connecting directly to downtown Tower City in one direction and to Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the east side, including University Circle, in the other. I-90 access is also nearby.

faq.q: Do Ohio City landlords accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It varies by landlord. Cleveland, including Ohio City, has no ordinance requiring landlords to accept vouchers, since Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law and the city hasn't passed its own. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in the area accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready.
